sections.paragraph:
ξενισμός, οῦ, ὁ (ξενίζω; Pla. et al.; Polyb. 15, 17, 1; Diod S 3, 33, 7; ins; Pr 15:17; TestAbr B 13 p. 117, 20 [Stone p. 82] ‘shelter’) a strong psychological reaction caused by someth. strange, astonishment ξενισμὸν παρεῖχεν ἡ καινότης αὐτοῦ the newness of it caused astonishment IEph 19:2.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

someth. someth. = something

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 684
